overall_structure.description:
A single, self-supporting deck bridge that spans 22 units along the north–south axis (y direction), 3 units wide along the east–west axis (x direction), and approximately 4 units deep (z direction) from deck top to the lowest chord. It is a twin side-truss, deck-type bridge:
      - Top deck: a continuous 3×22 array of Small Wooden Blocks forming a flat deck wide enough (3 units) to comfortably support a 2.5 × 2.5 cargo footprint.
      - Underframe: two longitudinal bottom chords (left and right under the deck edges) located 2 blocks below the deck, joined to the deck edges by periodic vertical posts and triangulated by diagonal Braces to form two side trusses.
      - Bottom transverse ties: cross-ties at the bottom level every few units to keep both side trusses acting together and to add torsional stiffness.
      The deck length of 22 ensures that, after placement, at least 1 unit of deck rests on each terrain top beyond the 20-unit gap. All under-deck members are trimmed so they lie entirely inside the gap region to prevent any collision with the terrain’s vertical faces.
overall_structure.functionality: The 3-wide deck gives a stable platform for the 2.5 × 2.5 × 1.5 cargo at the bridge center. The twin side trusses (top deck rows as top chords, bottom longitudinal rows as bottom chords, triangulated by braces) increase the bending stiffness and reduce midspan deflection. Bottom transverse ties link left and right trusses, increasing lateral and torsional stiffness, so the deck does not twist under the gradually increasing, centrally applied load. The 22-unit deck overhangs onto both sides so it can sit by gravity on the terrain tops (z=5), while all under-deck members end within |y| <= 9 so nothing collides with the terrain vertical faces at y=±10 (z<5). This arrangement yields a stable, passive bridge capable of spanning the gap and carrying load without any powered components.
overall_structure.assembly: Single-piece assembly (one sub-structure). Build the continuous 3×22 top deck first with the default stone block integrated at the deck center. Then add the two bottom chords strictly within |y| ≤ 9 (i.e., 18-unit lengths) under the deck edges, add periodic vertical posts to connect the top and bottom chords at chosen y positions, then add bottom transverse ties at the same y positions to couple the two side trusses. Finally, install diagonal Braces in alternating directions in each bay between posts along both sides to triangulate the side trusses. The result is one rigid bridge that can be placed at (x=0, y=0, z=7) and allowed to settle onto the terrain.
overall_structure.motion_control: None. This is a passive, static structure without powered components. Expected motion: the bridge falls gently from z≈7 to rest on the two terrain tops at z=5, with deck ends at approximately y≈±11 bearing on the terrain. No user input required.

sub_structure_1.name: 22×3 Deck with 18×2-Deep Twin Side-Truss Underframe
sub_structure_1.description:
A long, 3-wide deck beam with twin side trusses beneath its edges. The top deck spans 22 units (so 1-unit overhang onto each terrain top), while the bottom longitudinal chords and truss webbing are restricted to the gap region only. The side trusses consist of:
        - Top chords: the outer rows of the deck.
        - Bottom chords: left and right longitudinal rows 2 units below the deck edges, each 18 units long (y from −9 to +9).
        - Vertical posts: at periodic y-positions, 2-block-tall posts connect the top and bottom chords.
        - Diagonal braces: alternate-direction Braces between posts along each side, forming triangulated bays to stiffen the span.
        Bottom transverse ties at the same y-positions couple both side trusses to resist torsion and keep the deck flat under central load.
sub_structure_1.components:
- Default base: 1× Stone starting block (1×1×1, mass 0.25) used as the central deck block.
        - Small Wooden Block (1×1×1, mass 0.3):
          • Top deck: 3 × 22 = 66 blocks (one of these positions is occupied by the Stone base).
          • Bottom chords: 2 × 18 = 36 blocks (left and right, y from −9 to +9).
          • Vertical posts: 6 y-positions per side × 2 sides × 2 blocks tall = 24 blocks.
          • Bottom transverse ties: 6 blocks (one at x=0 for each chosen y-position).
          Approximate total wooden blocks: 66 + 36 + 24 + 6 = 132 wooden blocks (plus 1 stone).
        - Brace (connector, mass 0.5):
          • Side truss diagonals: 5 bays per side × 1 brace per bay × 2 sides = 10 Braces.
          (Optional: add 2–4 more braces for extra torsional coupling if needed; omit for lower cost in the base design.)
        - No Powered Wheels, Torches, Water Cannons, or Winches are used.
sub_structure_1.assembly:
Orientation and overall size:
        - Bridge long axis along y (north–south), width along x (east–west), depth along z (up–down).
        - Final deck planform: 22 (y) × 3 (x). Overall depth: about 4 units (1-unit deck thickness + 2-unit separation + 1-unit bottom chord thickness).

        Stepwise arrangement:
        1) Top deck (z top layer):
           - Start from the default Stone base as the central deck block at y≈0, x=0.
           - Build the center row along y to achieve a continuous 22-block row from y=−11 to y=+11.
           - Add left (x=−1) and right (x=+1) deck rows along the same y range to make a 3×22 deck.
        2) Bottom chords (z lower layer), within the gap only:
           - Left bottom chord: under the left deck row (x=−1), place a straight 18-block line from y=−9 to y=+9, 2 blocks below the top deck.
           - Right bottom chord: mirror under the right deck row (x=+1), same y range and depth.
           Important: keep all bottom chord blocks and any under-deck posts/diagonals within |y| ≤ 9 to avoid interference with the terrain’s vertical faces at y=±10.
        3) Vertical posts (connect top and bottom chords):
           - At y= −9, −5, −1, +1, +5, +9 (6 positions), at x=−1 and x=+1, stack two Small Wooden Blocks downward from the underside of the deck row so they reach and attach to the bottom chords. This yields 2-block-tall posts at each selected y.
        4) Bottom transverse ties:
           - At each of the same 6 y positions, place one Small Wooden Block at x=0 at the bottom-chord level to connect the inner faces of the left and right bottom chord blocks (the bottom chord blocks at x=±1 attach to the cross-tie’s ±x faces). These ties couple both side trusses and resist torsion.
        5) Diagonal Braces (triangulate the side trusses):
           - For each side (x=−1 and x=+1), between successive posts, install one Brace per bay in alternating directions (“N” pattern). For example, on the left side:
             • Bay (−9 to −5): Brace from the bottom chord at y=−9 up to the deck edge at y=−5.
             • Bay (−5 to −1): Brace from top at y=−5 down to bottom at y=−1.
             • Bay (−1 to +1): Brace from bottom at y=−1 up to top at y=+1.
             • Bay (+1 to +5): Brace from top at y=+1 down to bottom at y=+5.
             • Bay (+5 to +9): Brace from bottom at y=+5 up to top at y=+9.
           - Mirror the same alternating pattern on the right side.
           - Ensure each Brace connects between actual block faces (e.g., the underside of a top-deck edge block to the top face of the corresponding bottom-chord block in the next panel).
        6) Clearance check:
           - Confirm no bottom chord, post, or brace extends beyond |y|=9. The deck alone may extend to |y|=11 to sit on the terrain tops at y=±10 to ±∞. This prevents any under-deck element at z<5 from intersecting the terrain mass.
sub_structure_1.motion_control: None. The bridge is passive. After placement at (x=0, y=0, z=7), let it gently settle under gravity; its deck ends will rest on the terrain tops at z=5, with the underframe suspended within the gap.
sub_structure_1.function:
- The 3-wide deck provides sufficient platform area for the 2.5×2.5 cargo, ensuring it sits centered without tipping.
        - Twin side trusses (top edge rows as top chords, bottom edge rows as bottom chords) resist bending; diagonal Braces create triangular panels for stiffness, reducing midspan deflection under increasing cargo load.
        - Bottom transverse ties make the two side trusses act together and resist twisting when the load is not perfectly centered.
        - Deck overlength (22) ensures bearing onto both terrain tops, while the lower members are confined to |y| ≤ 9 to avoid terrain collision.
sub_structure_1.design_requirements:
- Deck must be exactly 22 units long (y from −11 to +11 relative to the central block) and 3 units wide (x=−1, 0, +1).
        - All under-deck members (bottom chords, posts, diagonal braces, and bottom ties) must lie within |y| ≤ 9 so that nothing at z < 5 intersects the terrain vertical faces at y=±10.
        - Vertical spacing between top deck and bottom chords should be 2 blocks to achieve a deep section (overall depth ≈ 4 blocks). Posts must be tall enough (2 blocks) to connect top and bottom chords.
        - Maintain continuous, face-to-face attachments between adjacent Small Wooden Blocks for structural integrity; Braces must connect between attachable faces of existing blocks only.
        - No powered components should be included to avoid unintended motion; the bridge must settle and remain static under load by gravity and friction.
        - Positions may be micro‑adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.
